title: parosela ordiae
intro: Parosela ordiae, also known as the Indian or wild sage, is a flowering plant that belongs to the family Lamiaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Parosela ordiae include its ability to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and improve digestion. Herbal preparations of Parosela ordiae can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
